title: astragalus diphtherites subsp. leucomallus
intro: Astragalus diphtherites subsp. leucomallus, also known as the Leucomallus milkvetch, is a subspecies of the Astragalus diphtherites plant, a type of legume in the pea family. It is a perennial herb native to North America, known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The Leucomallus milkvetch has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, capsule, or tincture, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional before consumption.
